title: Oryzopsis verrucosa
taxonomy: Oryzopsis verrucosa is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Oryzopsis verrucosa is further classified within the genus Oryzopsis. The taxonomy of Oryzopsis verrucosa has been studied to help understand its evolutionary relationships with other plant species. Oryzopsis verrucosa is a rare or little-known species, which can make its classification subject to ongoing research and revision.

intro: Oryzopsis verrucosa, also known as the hairy bent grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering several health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving symptoms associated with fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Oryzopsis verrucosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Oryzopsis verrucosa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
